en: I, Paul, myself entreat you, by the meekness and gentleness of Christ- I who am humble when face to face with you, but bold toward you when I am away!-
zh: 我保羅與你們見面的時候是謙卑的，不在你們那裡的時候，卻是放膽的。現在我親自以基督的謙遜溫柔勸你們，

en: I beg of you that when I am present I may not have to show boldness with such confidence as I count on showing against some who suspect us of walking according to the flesh.
zh: 求你們使我在你們那裡的時候，不要滿有自信地放膽對你們，我認為要以這樣的自信，勇敢地對付那些以為我們是照著世俗的標準 行事的人。

en: For though we walk in the flesh, we are not waging war according to the flesh.
zh: 我們雖然在世上行事，卻不是按照世俗的方式作戰，

en: For the weapons of our warfare are not of the flesh but have divine power to destroy strongholds. We destroy arguments
zh: 因為我們作戰所用的兵器，不是屬於這世界的，而是在 神面前有能力的，可以攻陷堅固的堡壘，

en: and every lofty opinion raised against the knowledge of God, and take every thought captive to obey Christ,
zh: 攻破詭辯，和做來阻擋人認識 神的一切高牆，並且把一切心意奪回來，順服基督。

en: being ready to punish every disobedience, when your obedience is complete.
zh: 我們已經準備好了，等你們完全順服的時候，就懲罰所有不順從的人。

en: Look at what is before your eyes. If anyone is confident that he is Christ's, let him remind himself that just as he is Christ's, so also are we.
zh: 你們看事情，只看表面。如果有人自信是屬於基督的，他就應該再想一想：他是屬於基督的，同樣，我們也是屬於基督的。

en: For even if I boast a little too much of our authority, which the Lord gave for building you up and not for destroying you, I will not be ashamed.
zh: 主把權柄賜給我們，是為了要造就你們，不是要破壞你們，就算我為這權柄誇口過分了一點，也不會覺得慚愧。

en: I do not want to appear to be frightening you with my letters.
zh: 不要以為我寫信是用來嚇你們。

en: For they say, "His letters are weighty and strong, but his bodily presence is weak, and his speech of no account."
zh: 因為有人說：“他的信又嚴厲又強硬，他本人卻其貌不揚，言語粗俗。”

en: Let such a person understand that what we say by letter when absent, we do when present.
zh: 說這話的人要想一想，我們不在你們那裡的時候，信上怎樣寫，我們來到的時候也會怎樣作。

en: Not that we dare to classify or compare ourselves with some of those who are commending themselves. But when they measure themselves by one another and compare themselves with one another, they are without understanding.
zh: 我們不敢和那些自我推薦的人相提並論；他們拿自己來量自己，拿自己來比自己，實在不大聰明。

en: But we will not boast beyond limits, but will boast only with regard to the area of influence God assigned to us, to reach even to you.
zh: 我們所誇的，並沒有越過範圍，而是在 神量給我們的界限之內；這界限一直延伸到你們那裡。

en: For we are not overextending ourselves, as though we did not reach you. We were the first to come all the way to you with the gospel of Christ.
zh: 如果我們沒有到過你們那裡，現在就自誇得過分了；但事實上，我們早就把基督的福音傳到你們那裡了。

en: We do not boast beyond limit in the labors of others. But our hope is that as your faith increases, our area of influence among you may be greatly enlarged,
zh: 我們並沒有越過範圍，拿別人的勞苦來誇口。我們盼望隨著你們信心的增長，我們的界限就因你們而大大擴展，

en: so that we may preach the gospel in lands beyond you, without boasting of work already done in another's area of influence.
zh: 使我們可以把福音傳到你們以外的地方。別人在他的界限以內所完成的工作，我們不會拿來誇口的。

en: "Let the one who boasts, boast in the Lord."
zh: 誇口的應當靠著主誇口，

en: For it is not the one who commends himself who is approved, but the one whom the Lord commends.
zh: 因為蒙悅納的，不是自我推薦的人，而是主所推薦的人。
